This season is part of a long-term trend toward more frequent, more devastating fires in the West that shows no sign of slowing down.
As the climate warms, the fire season, which traditionally peaks in late summer and into the fall, has been extending. https://nyti.ms/3kIyHYM
In California, some of 2020’s largest fires were close to areas that burned in recent years — again threatening lives and homes, and putting fragile ecosystems back at risk. http://nyti.ms/3kIyHYM
The fires have become more destructive over time, especially as people have moved further into fire-prone areas. http://nyti.ms/3kIyHYM
A thick blanket of smoke and soot spread across the region as wind fueled many of the fires in the last month, forcing millions of people to breathe polluted air. http://nyti.ms/3kIyHYM
